title: ferray-strings: no is* classification functions (isalpha, isdigit, isspace, etc.)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's char module (via defchararray.py chararray methods) provides: isalpha, isdigit, isspace, isalnum, isdecimal, isnumeric, istitle, isupper, islower. These return boolean arrays and are useful for data validation and cleaning. ferray-strings has none of these. Rust's str methods (is_ascii_alphabetic, etc.) make implementation straightforward.
